prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Spawn three subagents in parallel, each with a different stance. Each reads my curated

risks_fired.evidence: Multi-step orchestration prompt ('Spawn... Then synthesize... Append... Show me before saving') invites planning narration before any subagent is dispatched.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Prefix 'No preamble — start dispatching immediately' to suppress orchestration narration from eating the student's highest-attention slot.

risks_fired.evidence: Append an `## Answer` section to ./crux.md — no explicit 'don't touch existing sections' boundary.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add 'Append only — do not modify any existing sections of ./crux.md' immediately after the append instruction.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Show me before saving' provides the pre-confirm; risk is largely neutralised but residual on a file with existing content.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Existing 'Show me before saving' is the right mitigation; no change needed for this pattern.

risks_fired.evidence: 'don't smooth the disagreement' explicitly guards; low residual risk on the synthesis step.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Existing guard is adequate; no change needed.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: You choose fixes. Aim for optimal function in the next session that runs on this.

risks_fired.evidence: 'You choose fixes' delegates curation to Claude on work it produced; Claude will prefer cosmetic fixes and under-flag structural issues.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Optional residual prompt by design; if hardened, add 'over-flag — assume worse than it looks; name structural issues before cosmetic ones'.

risks_fired.evidence: Open-ended 'you choose' may elicit a short planning narration; risk is low given the prompt's brevity.
risks_fired.fix_hint: No change needed for an intentional optional residual prompt.
